hero.subtitle: PsyMind.AI is your companion for High School and College exams. Organize your routine, control anxiety and achieve your goals with AI support.

study_focus.title: Study Focus
study_focus.description: Organization tips and personalized schedules for national and college exams.

anxiety_control.title: Anxiety Control
anxiety_control.description: Breathing and mindfulness techniques to stay calm before tests and essays.

always_available.title: Available 24/7
always_available.description: Ask questions, vent or ask for study tips at any time of day or night.

gemini.title: Powered by Google Gemini
gemini.description: PsyMind.AI uses <1>Google Gemini 1.5 Flash</1>, a next-generation AI model, to offer empathetic and scientifically grounded answers.

api.title: Gemini API
api.description: Direct integration with the Google Gemini API for natural and contextualized conversations.

notebooklm.title: NotebookLM
notebooklm.description: Prompt engineering developed with NotebookLM, based on educational psychology studies.

gems.title: Gemini Gems
gems.description: Personality and tone of voice optimized through Gems for teenage emotional support.

education.title: Educational Focus
education.description: PsyMind.AI isn't just a chat, it's a journey companion for <1>Middle and High School</1> students.

stress.title: Stress Management
stress.description: Support to deal with the pressure of exams, mock tests and career choices.

mentoring.title: College Mentoring
mentoring.description: Strategic guidance for national and main college exams, keeping focus on results.

self_knowledge.title: Self-knowledge
self_knowledge.description: Tools that help in discovering vocations and emotional maturation.

license.title: Transparency and Open Source

transparency.title: Transparency

kindness.title: Acts of Kindness
kindness.loading: AI is finding the perfect act...
kindness.success_toast: Congratulations! The world is a little bit better.

prompts.random: Suggest a simple and practical act of kindness anyone can do today (1 sentence).
prompts.stranger: Suggest a simple act of kindness to do for a stranger (1 sentence).
prompts.family: Suggest a simple act of kindness to do for family or friends (1 sentence).
prompts.self: Suggest a simple act of self-care or self-compassion (1 sentence).
prompts.work: Suggest a simple act of kindness to do at work or school (1 sentence).

stranger: Sincerely compliment the clothing or accessory of someone you don't know.
Hold the door for someone entering or exiting.
Leave a positive note in a library book or public place.
Smile and greet the bus driver or supermarket cashier.
Pay for the coffee of the person behind you in line (if you can).
family: Call a relative just to tell them you love them.
Do a household chore that isn't your responsibility.
Write a thank you letter to someone who helped you in the past.
Cook the favorite meal of someone in your house.
Actively listen to someone without interrupting or looking at your phone.
self: List 3 things you like about yourself.
Take 15 minutes to do something you love without guilt.
Forgive yourself for a mistake made recently.
Take a mindful walk observing nature.
Write in a journal how you are feeling today.
work: Offer help to a colleague who seems overwhelmed.
Praise someone's work publicly or to the boss.
Bring a snack or sweet to share with the team.
Leave an encouraging post-it on someone's desk.
Thank someone for something specific they did.

about.title: About PsyMindAI
about.text: PsyMindAI is a virtual assistant focused on mental health and well-being. Our goal is to provide a safe space for conversations, study tips, and emotional support.

chat.label: Start a conversation
chat.desc: Type your message in the text box below and press Enter or click the send button.

new.label: New Chat
new.desc: Use the 'New chat' button in the sidebar to start a new topic.

settings.label: Customize
settings.desc: Access settings to adjust theme, font size, and accessibility options.

support.label: Help Line
support.desc: Access the Help Line in the tools menu for immediate emotional support.

q1.question: Does PsyMindAI substitute a psychologist?
q1.answer: No. PsyMindAI is an educational support tool and does not substitute professional companion. If you're in crisis, please seek professional help immediately.

q2.question: Are my conversations private?
q2.answer: Yes, your conversations are processed locally and we prioritize your privacy. No personal info is shared.

q3.question: How does the Pomodoro work?
q3.answer: The Pomodoro technique helps you focus. The default is 25m focus and 5m break. You can activate it in the tools menu.

q4.question: Can I use it offline?
q4.answer: Currently PsyMindAI requires internet connection to process answers.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Speed up your navigation with these quick shortcuts.

feedback.title: Your opinion matters
feedback.desc: Help us improve PsyMindAI by sending suggestions, compliments, or reporting issues.
feedback.success_desc: Thank you for helping us improve PsyMindAI.

default_quotes.text: It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.

default_quotes.text: Know thyself and thou shalt know all the mysteries of the gods and of the universe.

default_quotes.text: Success consists of going from failure to failure without loss of enthusiasm.

telemetry.title: Improve PsyMind.AI
telemetry.description:
📊 Anonymous usage data helps improve PsyMind.AI.

🔒 No conversations or personal information are collected.
Everything stays on your device.

You can disable this at any time.
telemetry.warning: Disclaimer: The AI provides basic educational and emotional support, but does not replace the care of a psychologist or mental health professional.

mode_simple.title: Simple Average
mode_simple.desc: I already have my final grades and want to calculate the average.

mode_advanced.title: By Hits
mode_advanced.desc: I only have the answer key and want to estimate my score.

calculator.ignore_redacao: I don't have the essay score yet

result.warning: The Essay score WAS NOT included in this calculation. This average considers only the 4 objective areas.
result.note_simple: *This is a simple average. SiSU and ProUni may use different weights depending on the course and university.
result.note_advanced: *This is an estimate based on previous patterns. The real score depends on IRT (Item Response Theory) and question difficulty.

prompts.simple:
🎓 ADMISSION FEASIBILITY ANALYSIS

📊 MY TARGET SCORES:
• Languages: {{linguagens}}
• Human Sciences: {{humanas}}
• Natural Sciences: {{natureza}}
• Mathematics: {{matematica}}
• Essay: {{redacao}}

📈 Simple Average: {{avg}}

🎯 GOAL:
• Course: {{course}}
• Category/Modality: {{category}}

Please help me with:

1. 🏛️ VIABLE UNIVERSITIES
   - List universities where I have a GOOD chance
   - Mention recent cutoff scores

2. ⚠️ RISKY OPTIONS
   - Universities where it would be harder, but possible

3. ⚖️ WEIGHT SYSTEM
   - How does {{course}} usually weigh these areas?
   - What is my estimated weighted score?

4. 💡 STRATEGIES
   - Should I focus on improving any specific area?
   - Admission application tips

5. 🧠 EMOTIONAL SUPPORT
   - How to manage waiting anxiety?
   - Personalized motivational message

Be realistic but encouraging! 💪

prompts.plan:
Create a complete study plan for {{exam}} - {{subject}}:

📚 TOPICS:
{{topics}}

Please provide:
1. Ideal study order
2. Suggested time for each topic
3. Specific study techniques
4. Recommended resources
5. How this content appears in exams
6. Memorization and review tips

Be practical and motivating! 💪
prompts.strategy:
Analyze the preparation strategy for {{exam}} - {{subject}}:

🎯 How should I prioritize my studies?
📊 Which topics are most frequent?
⚡ Quick resolution techniques
🧠 How to handle pre-exam anxiety
📝 Time management tips during the exam

Give practical and motivating advice!
prompts.explain:
Clearly and didactically explain the following topic of {{subject}} for {{exam}}:

📌 {{topic}}

Including:
1. Fundamental concepts
2. Practical examples
3. How it appears on exams
4. Memorization tips
5. Common mistakes to avoid

Use accessible language! 📚

default_trails.title: Anxiety Management
default_trails.description: Learn to identify and manage anxiety symptoms.

content.front: What is the 'Fight or Flight' response?
content.back: It's a physiological reaction that occurs in response to a perceived harmful event, attack, or threat to survival, activating the sympathetic nervous system.

content.question: Which breathing technique is most recommended to reduce acute anxiety?

content.back: The act of bringing your attention to the present moment, focusing on physical senses (e.g., 5 things you see, 4 you touch).

default_trails.title: Nonviolent Communication
default_trails.description: Improve your relationships by communicating empathetically.

content.front: What are the 4 components of NVC?

content.question: Which of these phrases is an example of an 'Observation' (without judgment) in NVC?

options.text: I noticed you arrived 10 minutes late to the last meetings.

default_trails.title: Emotional Intelligence
default_trails.description: Recognize and control your own emotions.

content.back: The ability to recognize and understand your own moods, emotions, and drives.

content.question: A key pillar of emotional intelligence in the workplace is:

default_trails.title: Mindfulness and Focus
default_trails.description: Mindfulness techniques to stay focused on the present.

content.back: A mental state achieved by focusing one's awareness on the present moment, while calmly acknowledging and accepting one's feelings.

content.question: When getting distracted during meditation, you should:

options.text: Give up and try another day.
Notice the distraction and gently return focus to the breath.

default_trails.title: Cognitive Biases
default_trails.description: Understand the thinking errors that affect our decisions.

content.back: The tendency to search for and remember information that confirms one's preconceptions.

options.text: Overestimating one's own ability in tasks where one has little experience.
The habit of buying things on sale.

default_trails.title: Study Organization
default_trails.description: Time management and school planning techniques.

content.back: A time management method that alternates intense focus blocks (e.g., 25 mins) with short breaks (e.g., 5 mins) to maintain productivity.

content.question: What is the best way to prepare for a week with multiple exams?

options.text: Study everything the night before the exam.
Create a schedule distributing the subjects throughout the week.
Don't study and rely only on what you remember from classes.

default_trails.title: School Pressure and Exams
default_trails.description: Strategies to manage pre-exam nervousness and avoid going 'blank'.

content.front: What causes you to go 'blank' during a test?
content.back: Going 'blank' is usually caused by an anxiety spike. The brain perceives the exam as an immediate threat, blocking memory access.

content.question: What to do if you feel you're going to go blank during an exam?

options.text: Take a break, close your eyes, take a deep breath, and drink water if possible.
Answer anything as quickly as possible.

content.back: Often pressure comes from parents' or teachers' expectations. Remembering that a bad grade does not define your intelligence is key to reducing and dealing with this excessive pressure.

immediate.title: How are you feeling today?
immediate.desc: Our AI can suggest exercises and resources based on your emotional state.
immediate.placeholder: Ex: I'm feeling very anxious about an exam tomorrow...
immediate.chat: I want to talk more about this

general.msg: I understand you are going through this. Here are some resources that might help:

anxiety.msg: It sounds like you're feeling anxious. Let's try to calm down together.

res2.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique
res2.desc: Identify things around you to ground yourself

sadness.msg: I'm so sorry you're feeling this way. You are not alone.

res1.title: Self-Compassion
res1.desc: Be gentle with yourself right now

res2.title: Connection
res2.desc: Consider talking to someone you trust

crisis.msg: You are important and help is available. Please reach out to professionals now.

immediate.prompt:
Analyze this feeling: "{{feeling}}". Identify the main emotion (anxiety, sadness, anger, etc) and suggest 2 quick emotional support techniques (1 sentence each). Format: EMOÇÃO: [name]
TÉCNICA 1: [title] - [description]
TÉCNICA 2: [title] - [description]

step1.title: Which area of life does this affect the most right now?
step1.naosei: I don't know how to identify

step2.title: How long have you felt this way?
step2.sempre: It feels like it's always this way

investigate.prompt:
Analyze: Emotion={{emotion}}, Context={{context}}, Duration={{duration}}. Provide: TÍTULO: [name]
DESCRIÇÃO: [2 sentences]
CONSELHO: [1 practical sentence]

general.title: Initial Analysis
general.desc: Based on what you shared, it seems you are facing a challenging time.
general.advice: We recommend focusing on short breaks and self-care.

anxiety_studies.title: Academic Anxiety
anxiety_studies.desc: Feeling pressure with studies is common. Anxiety can come from the fear of not being able to cope.
anxiety_studies.advice: Try dividing the content into smaller parts (Pomodoro) and remember that your value is not your grade.

anxiety_future.title: Anticipatory Anxiety
anxiety_future.desc: Worrying about the future is natural, but it can paralyze.
anxiety_future.advice: Focus on what you can control today. The future is built one day at a time.

sadness.title: Embracing Sadness
sadness.desc: Sadness is a valid emotion and signals that something needs attention.
sadness.advice: Allow yourself to feel without judgment. Writing about your feelings can help process them.

tiredness.title: Mental Exhaustion
tiredness.desc: You might be close to a burnout if you don't rest.
tiredness.advice: Rest is productive. Disconnect from screens and do something that doesn't require mental effort.

investigate.chat_prompt: Hi, PsyMind. I'm going through a delicate moment and would like to vent. I have been feeling {{emotion}} regarding {{context}}. This has affected me {{duration}}. Could you listen to me and help me better understand what I'm feeling?

step0.title: Cognitive Reframing
step0.desc: Let's work together to identify and reframe negative thoughts.

step1.title: Challenge your Thoughts
step1.desc: Identify a negative thought and we will work together to find a more balanced perspective.
step1.q_situ_placeholder: Ex: I received criticism at work...
step1.q_thought_placeholder: Ex: I am incompetent and I'll be fired...
step1.q_intensity: How does this make you feel? (0-10)

reframing.prompt:
Analyze this negative thought using CBT:
Situation: {{situation}}
Thought: {{thought}}

Provide:
DISTORÇÃO: [type of cognitive distortion]
DESAFIO: [1 sentence explaining the distortion]
ALTERNATIVA: [1 sentence with alternative perspective]
REFORMULAÇÃO: [new balanced thought]

general.challenge: You are taking an isolated event as a universal pattern or predicting the worst possible scenario without concrete evidence.
general.alternative: Try to view the situation as a single event and not as a definition of who you are. Ask yourself: 'What is the real evidence that this will always happen?'
general.reframe: Instead of thinking '{{thought}}', try thinking: 'Although this situation was difficult, it does not define my entire future or capability.'

all_or_nothing.challenge: Life is rarely black or white. Using 'always' or 'never' usually indicates a distortion of reality.
all_or_nothing.alternative: Look for exceptions. Have there been times when this didn't happen? Try using words like 'sometimes' or 'frequently'.

should_statements.challenge: Using 'should' creates guilt and frustration. You are imposing rigid rules on yourself or others.
should_statements.alternative: Replace 'I should' with 'I would like' or 'It would be preferable'. This reduces pressure and increases acceptance.

support.chat_feeling_prompt: Hi, PsyMind. I'm going through a delicate moment and would like to vent. {{feeling}}. Could you listen to me and help me better understand what I'm feeling?

footer.brand_desc: Your study and emotional well-being companion, available 24/7 to help you evolve.
footer.disclaimer: PsyMind.AI offers basic educational and emotional support. For serious mental health issues, always seek specialized professional help.
